title: Optimizing Workload Migration for Carbon and Cost Reductions Under Grid Constraints: New Insights and a Practical Evaluation Framework
authors: Can Hankendi, Ayse K. Coskun
summary: Introduces a practical evaluation framework for shifting AI workloads across sites while balancing grid constraints, carbon reductions, and operating cost.

title: Investigating Power Consumption Flexibility of AI Data Centers for Demand Response Participation
authors: Fatih Acun, Can Hankendi, Ethan Levine, Hudson Reynolds, Joshua Bardwick, Ayse K. Coskun
summary: Studies how AI data centers can expose flexible load to the grid and quantifies the operational tradeoffs of participating in demand response programs.

title: Why transparency matters for sustainable data centers and carbon-neutral artificial intelligence (AI)
authors: Can Hankendi, Ayse K. Coskun, Benjamin K. Sovacool
summary: Argues that transparent reporting of workload, energy, and carbon metrics is essential for credible sustainability claims in AI infrastructure.

title: Lessons Learned from Anomaly Detection in Chameleon Cloud
authors: S. M. Qasim, C. Hankendi, M. Sherman, K. Keahey, G. Stringhini, A. K. Coskun
venue: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Cloud Engineering (IC2E)
summary: Distills operational lessons from deploying anomaly detection pipelines in a real cloud environment and highlights practical reliability challenges.

title: Data center demand response for sustainable computing: Myth or opportunity?
authors: Ayse K. Coskun, Fatih Acun, Quentin Clark, Can Hankendi, Daniel C. Wilson
venue: Design, Automation & Test in Europe Conference & Exhibition (DATE)
summary: Evaluates whether demand response can become a meaningful systems lever for sustainable data center operation rather than a niche grid service.

title: Power management for heterogeneous computing systems
authors: Can Hankendi, Manish Arora, Indrani Paul
summary: Describes mechanisms for coordinating power management policies across heterogeneous compute resources.

title: Determining thermal time constants of processing systems
authors: Can Hankendi, Manish Arora, Indrain Paul, Wei Huang, Srilatha Manne
summary: Presents techniques for characterizing thermal response behavior in processing systems to inform control and design decisions.

title: Scale & Cap: Scaling-Aware Resource Management for Consolidated Multi-threaded Applications
authors: Can Hankendi, Ayse Kivilcim Coskun
venue: ACM Transactions on Design Automation of Electronic Systems (TODAES)
summary: Proposes a scaling-aware runtime for resource allocation and power capping in consolidated multi-threaded workloads.

title: Adapt&Cap: Coordinating System and Application-level Adaptation for Power Constrained Systems
authors: Can Hankendi, Henry Hoffmann, Ayse Coskun
summary: Shows how coordinated adaptation across the OS and application stack improves behavior under strict power constraints.

title: Exploiting limited context streams
authors: Manish Arora, Can Hankendi, Syed Ali R. Jafri, Andrew G. Kegel
summary: Explores architectural mechanisms for extracting additional value from constrained hardware context streams.

title: Adaptive runtime techniques for power and resource management on multi-core systems
authors: Can Hankendi
summary: Dissertation on runtime techniques for jointly managing energy, performance, and shared resources on multicore systems.

title: Message passing-aware power management on many-core systems
authors: Andrea Bartolini, Can Hankendi, Ayse Kivilcim Coskun, Luca Benini
summary: Incorporates communication behavior into power management decisions for many-core systems.

title: Adapt&Cap: A Framework for Unifying System and Application-level Adaptive Management
authors: Can Hankendi, Henry Hoffmann, Ayse K. Coskun
summary: Introduces an early framework for coordinating system and application adaptation under shared power constraints.

title: Autonomous Resource Sharing for Multi-Threaded Workloads in Virtualized Servers
authors: Can Hankendi, Ayse K. Coskun
summary: Examines autonomous resource sharing policies for improving performance efficiency in virtualized multi-threaded servers.

title: vCap: Adaptive power capping for virtualized servers
authors: Can Hankendi, Sherief Reda, Ayse K. Coskun
venue: International Symposium on Low Power Electronics and Design (ISLPED)
summary: Presents an adaptive power capping framework for virtualized servers that balances energy limits with workload needs.

title: Energy-efficient server consolidation for multi-threaded applications in the cloud
authors: Can Hankendi, Ayse K. Coskun
summary: Studies consolidation strategies for cloud workloads with an emphasis on multi-threaded application behavior and energy efficiency.

title: Adaptive power and resource management techniques for multi-threaded workloads
authors: Can Hankendi, Ayse K. Coskun
venue: 2013 IEEE International Symposium on Parallel & Distributed Processing Workshops and PhD Forum (IPDPSW)
summary: Surveys adaptive techniques for managing performance and power in multi-threaded workloads.

title: Dynamic server power capping for enabling data center participation in power markets
authors: Hao Chen, Can Hankendi, Michael C. Caramanis, Ayse K. Coskun
venue: 2013 IEEE/ACM International Conference on Computer-Aided Design (ICCAD)
summary: Connects runtime server power control with electricity market participation for data center operators.

title: Reducing the energy cost of computing through efficient co-scheduling of parallel workloads
authors: Can Hankendi, Ayse K. Coskun
venue: 2012 Design, Automation & Test in Europe Conference & Exhibition (DATE)
summary: Shows how co-scheduling can cut the energy cost of parallel workloads without sacrificing system throughput.

title: Adaptive energy-efficient resource sharing for multi-threaded workloads in virtualized systems
authors: Can Hankendi, A. Coskun
venue: International Workshop on Computing in Heterogeneous, Autonomous' N' Goal-oriented Environments (DAC-CHANGE)
summary: Explores energy-aware resource sharing techniques for virtualized platforms running multi-threaded workloads.

title: Software optimization for performance, energy, and thermal distribution: Initial case studies
authors: Md Ashfaquzzaman Khan, Can Hankendi, Ayse Kivilcim Coskun, Martin C. Herbordt
venue: 2011 International Green Computing Conference and Workshops (IGCC)
summary: Presents early case studies on jointly optimizing software for performance, energy use, and thermal behavior.

title: Identifying the optimal energy-efficient operating points of parallel workloads
authors: Ryan Cochran, Can Hankendi, Ayse Coskun, Sherief Reda
venue: 2011 IEEE/ACM International Conference on Computer-Aided Design (ICCAD)
summary: Identifies operating points that minimize energy for parallel workloads while accounting for system-level performance tradeoffs.

title: Pack & cap: adaptive DVFS and thread packing under power caps
authors: Ryan Cochran, Can Hankendi, Ayse K. Coskun, Sherief Reda
venue: Proceedings of the 44th Annual IEEE/ACM International Symposium on Microarchitecture (MICRO)
summary: Combines DVFS and thread packing to maintain performance efficiency under strict power caps.
